Sake Dinner Featuring Heiwa Shuzo
events.description: Join us for a guided evening featuring 6 courses paired with 6 premium sakes from Heiwa Shuzo, an acclaimed brewery from Wakayama, Japan. Founded over 100 years ago, Heiwa Shuzo blends traditional brewing methods with a modern approach that has earned international recognition for its KID sake lineup. Throughout the dinner, sake sommelier Tayler Pecha will lead guests through each pairing, covering tasting notes, brewing techniques, rice polishing, and the story behind the brewery. Whether you're new to sake or already a fan, this is a great opportunity to explore a variety of styles in a relaxed and educational setting. events.description: Come join us at High-Hand Brewery to hear a live performance by Manzanita. They are a Indie Folk band from Northern California with blends of Americana and Country. A Manzanita performance is a huge sound from two men. Aaron Shively’s pedal steel playing is seasoned and tasteful with a ‘Hammond organ-like’ tone. His mandolin will delight any bluegrass lover. Original songwriting with storytelling, catchy vocal melodies and powerful harmonies give this duo a classic and eclectic sound all can enjoy. You can learn more about them by clicking here to visit their website. Only guests 18 and older may attend our “On Stage” concerts.

A multi-instrumentalist who began learning guitar under the guidance of his uncle and mentor David Bowman, Scott has honed his craft across guitar, bass, drums, and piano—often recording entire projects on his own. Blending heartfelt storytelling with the timeless spirit of 80s and 90s country, Christopher Scott delivers melodic songs that connect with audiences and keep them singing along. A true independent artist, he brings an authentic love for country music and its roots to every performance. You can learn more about him by clicking here to visit their website. Sacramento’s ultimate time machine to the golden age of rock — this powerhouse band slams through the best of 90’s and early 2000’s alternative, grunge, and hard-hitting rock with raw energy and a rebel soul. Fronted by both the gritty vocals of Jon Grant and Owen Myers, the band features their ripping guitar work and keyboard melodies, respectively. Guitarists Alex Schmidt and Brian Ilagan shred with precision and passion, while Dave Wong lays down the thunder on bass and Gar Garvey keeps the heartbeat pounding on drums. From the anthems of The Smashing Pumpkins, Pearl Jam and Alice In Chains to the pulse of Foo Fighters, CAKE, Bush and more, 120 Minutes doesn’t just play the soundtrack of a generation—they ignite it. This band is a must-see for anyone who ever wore Doc Martens, blasted their car stereo, or just wanted to rock without apology. Get ready to relive the era when guitars ruled, angst was an art form, and every track hit like a shot of adrenaline. 120 Minutes brings it all back—louder, tighter, and harder than ever. Are you ready to feel it again? You can learn more about them by clicking here to visit their website. Only guests 18 and older may attend our “On Stage” concerts.

With tight musicianship and a polished stage presence, this Sacramento-area band keeps the party going all night—perfect for anyone looking to sing along, hit the dance floor, and enjoy a fun, feel-good live show. events.title: On Stage with The Used Cars
events.description: Come join us at High-Hand Brewery to hear a live performance by The Used Cars. Step into a nostalgic journey through the iconic sounds of the 80s new wave synth-pop era with The Used Cars. Keeping the spirit of The Cars alive, this dynamic band faithfully delivers all the hits from the 1978 eponymous album to the 1984 release of Heartbeat City. With dual lead singers expertly channeling the essence of Ric Ocasek and Benjamin Orr, The Used Cars create an authentic experience that might make you believe you’re listening to the original legends. Their genuine passion for the music propels them to infuse an extra dose of magic into every performance, ensuring a night on the dance floor that’s exactly what you’ve been craving. You can learn more about them by clicking here to visit their website. Only guests 18 and older may attend our “On Stage” concerts.

events.title: On Stage with Halfway Inn
events.description: Come join us at High-Hand Brewery to hear a live performance by Halfway Inn. Prepare to be electrified by the dynamic energy of Halfway Inn, a powerhouse Hard Rock band that demands your attention. Their electrifying performances transport you through the greatest hits of past decades, igniting the stage with their relentless passion for music. A regular fixture in Sacramento’s vibrant music scene, Halfway Inn consistently leaves their mark on diverse venues across the region. Their live shows are nothing short of spectacular, as they pour their hearts and souls into each performance, captivating the audience with an unforgettable blend of raw talent and captivating showmanship. Don’t miss the chance to experience Halfway Inn in action – it’s not just a concert; it’s a musical journey that will leave you craving more. Here’s what you can expect… 70’s Vintage Hard Rock: UFO, THIN LIZZY, BAD COMPANY, LED ZEPPELIN, AC/DC, QUEEN,LYNYRD SKYNYRD 80’s Arena Rock: Y&T, EDDIE MONEY, DANZIG, SCORPIONS,IRON MAIDEN, POISON, BRYAN ADAMS, WHITESNAKE 90’s ALTERNATIVE/GRUNGE: PEARL JAM, THE TOADIES,BUSH, STP, LIVE, SEVEN MARY THREE 2000’s Bangers: BUCK CHERRY, FINGER ELEVEN, PUDDLE OF MUD, DEAD DAISIES AND MANY MORE! Only guests 18 and older may attend our “On Stage” concerts.

The Vintage Tees are a four‑piece powerhouse from Folsom, CA, featuring Will Kouvaris (guitar/vocals), Steve Crain (lead guitar), JJ Flores (bass), and Robert Lundstrom (drums). We light up the stage with a foot‑stompin’ blend of classic and southern rock, bluesy favorites, country‑tinged tunes, and beloved rock hits from the ’80s and ’90s. Whether you’re in the mood for Lynyrd Skynyrd, Led Zeppelin, Dwight Yoakam, or the Killers, we’ve got you covered — something for every music lover. events.title: On Stage with Manzanita
events.description: Come join us at High-Hand Brewery to hear a live performance by Manzanita. They are a Indie Folk band from Northern California with blends of Americana and Country. A Manzanita performance is a huge sound from two men. Aaron Shively’s pedal steel playing is seasoned and tasteful with a ‘Hammond organ-like’ tone. His mandolin will delight any bluegrass lover. Nick Foster fronts with his parlor-style acoustic guitar, harmonica, kick drum and tambourine simultaneously. Original songwriting with storytelling, catchy vocal melodies and powerful harmonies give this duo a classic and eclectic sound all can enjoy. You can learn more about them by clicking here to visit their website. Only guests 18 and older may attend our “On Stage” concerts.

When you arrive, find Melissa to sign up and lock in your spot. High-Hand’s pro backline will be ready for you. Solo players will be paired up into spontaneous jam groups, and together you’ll decide what to play—keeping it loose, creative, and fun. It’s all about rocking out, connecting with fellow musicians, and celebrating the amazing music community we’re surrounded by. Each week will also feature a guest band to keep the night energized and inspiring. Come jam, hang out, and make your Wednesday groove. Only guests 18 and older may attend our “On Stage” concerts.

Every Wednesday, we’re opening the stage to musicians of all kinds—bring your axe, your voice, your full band, or just the urge to play. When you arrive, find Melissa to sign up and lock in your spot. High-Hand’s pro backline will be ready for you. Solo players will be paired up into spontaneous jam groups, and together you’ll decide what to play—keeping it loose, creative, and fun. It’s all about rocking out, connecting with fellow musicians, and celebrating the amazing music community we’re surrounded by. Each week will also feature a guest band to keep the night energized and inspiring. Come jam, hang out, and make your Wednesday groove. Only guests 18 and older may attend our “On Stage” concerts.
